articles.title: Brand Creates Exclusive Collectible Cans in celebration of Disney’s “Lilo & Stitch” Live-Action Movie
articles.summary: The SPAM® brand has announced a collaboration with Disney's upcoming live-action "Lilo & Stitch" movie, releasing exclusive Hawaiian-inspired limited-edition collectible cans featuring artwork from the film. This partnership also includes a new TV commercial campaign starting May 5, celebrating the cultural connection both the SPAM® brand and Stitch share with Hawaiian traditions and family. The collectible cans are available nationwide, with the movie set to premiere in theaters on May 23, 2025.

articles.title: The Walt Disney Company To Participate In The Moffettnathanson Media, Internet & Communications Conference
articles.summary: The Walt Disney Company announced that Josh D’Amaro, Chairman, Disney Experiences, will participate in a question-and-answer session at the MoffettNathanson Media, Internet & Communications Conference. The session is scheduled for Wednesday, May 14, 2025, at approximately 9:40 a.m. ET/ 6:40 a.m. PT. A live stream will be available on the company's investor relations website, where an archived recording will also be accessible.

articles.title: The Walt Disney Company Launches Inaugural, Company-Wide “Disney Week Of Wishes” Campaign To Celebrate World Wish Day With Make-A-Wish
articles.summary: The Walt Disney Company has launched its first-ever "Disney Week of Wishes" campaign to celebrate World Wish Day on April 29 and its 45-year partnership with Make-A-Wish. This week-long event highlights Disney's unique ability to grant wishes, with special moments like a Princess-themed ball, meeting Marvel stars, and a wish-granting moment on American Idol. Disney, the world's largest wish granter for Make-A-Wish, aims to bring happiness and hope to children with critical illnesses globally, having granted over 165,000 wishes since 1981.

articles.title: Jim Cramer Says The Walt Disney Company (DIS) ‘Just Has Been A One-way Trip To The Danger Zone Since Nelson Peltz Was No Longer Involved’
articles.summary: Jim Cramer commented on The Walt Disney Company (DIS), stating that the stock has been in a "danger zone" since activist investor Nelson Peltz was no longer involved. He expressed a prior belief that Peltz's presence on the board would have fostered operational discipline, a prediction he felt was validated by the stock's subsequent decline. Cramer also shared insights on the broader market, attributing a rally to President Trump's clarification that he would not fire Fed Chair Jerome Powell, and discussed the evolving perception of market recoveries.

articles.title: Hasbro’s stock rallies as toy maker ups its revenue forecast for ‘Wizards of the Coast’
articles.summary: Hasbro's stock rallied after the company exceeded first-quarter expectations and increased its revenue forecast for "Wizards of the Coast." The toy maker now anticipates its "Wizards" business to boost 2025 company revenue by mid-to-high teens, driven by new gaming sets under the Universes Beyond theme. This positive outlook follows a licensing deal for Disney's Star Wars and Marvel franchises.
